page_title: Paid family and sick leave — Missouri
meta_title: Missouri Paid Family and Sick Leave 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Missouri has no state paid family leave and no mandatory sick leave law after HB 567 repealed Prop A on 28 August 2025. FMLA covers employers with 50+ employees. Here's what you actually owe.
h1: Missouri paid family and sick leave
secondary_keywords: missouri proposition a sick leave repeal
hero_alt: People working in a modern office — paid leave and workplace rights in Missouri

factual_uncertainties.issue: Unused Prop A accruals post-repeal payout obligation
factual_uncertainties.detail: Missouri DOL did not publish definitive guidance on whether employers must pay out or honour Prop A sick leave accruals earned between 1 May and 27 August 2025 after the repeal took effect. Missouri Chamber of Commerce FAQ noted 'no guidance' as of July 2025.
factual_uncertainties.source: mochamber.com FAQ; Fisher Phillips advisory July 2025
factual_uncertainties.risk: low — affects only employers who had Missouri hires during May–August 2025 window with accrual tracking
factual_uncertainties.action: Legal review recommended for affected employers; flagged in HIGH_CONCERN.md

prompt_corrections.original: Verify status post any 2025 legislative repeal-attempt activity
prompt_corrections.verified: Repeal confirmed. RSMo 290.600 repealed by H.B. 567, effective 28 August 2025. Governor Kehoe signed 10 July 2025. No state replacement law enacted.

prompt_corrections.original: small employers (≤15) cap 40 hrs/year; large (15+) cap 56 hrs/year
prompt_corrections.verified: Confirmed. Small = fewer than 15 employees, 40 hrs/year cap. Large = 15 or more employees, 56 hrs/year cap. Both caps were operative during the 1 May–27 Aug 2025 window only.

flag_detail: factual_uncertainty: Prop A accrued time payout guidance was not published by Missouri DOL before repeal effective date
